content.subtitle: Use
content.description: Certain verbs only require a subject to form a complete sentence. No object is needed.

content.subtitle: Use
content.description: A large number of verbs take a single object after them to complete their meaning.

content.subtitle: Use
content.description: Certain verbs can take two objects at the same time. One is the direct object (the thing) and the other is the indirect object (usually a person).

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: Two prepositions are commonly placed between the direct object and the indirect object: **to** and **for**.
items: She bought a gift **for** her brother.

content.description: These verbs can also be used in the passive.
items: A promotion was offered to Tom.

content.subtitle: Be careful
items: When the indirect object is placed before the direct object, do not add a preposition.
Correct: She gave Leo the keys.
Incorrect: She gave to Leo the keys.

sections.title: A: Complete with the correct form
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with one suitable verb.

items.sentence: The baby was tired and soon ...... .
I ...... my keys on the table before I left.
Can you ...... me your dictionary for a minute?
We ...... the window because it was cold.
The children ...... when they heard the joke.
She ...... her friend a birthday card.

sections.title: B: Choose the correct sentence
sections.instructions: Choose the sentence that is grammatically correct.

items.sentence: A Simon gave Jill some flowers. / B Simon gave to Jill some flowers.
A I bought for my brother a present. / B I bought my brother a present.
A She showed us her new phone. / B She showed to us her new phone.
A They sent to me an email. / B They sent me an email.
A He read the story to the class. / B He read to the class the story.
A I wrote my teacher a message. / B I wrote to my teacher a message.

sections.title: C: Rewrite with to/for
sections.instructions: Rewrite each sentence using the structure with to or for.

items.sentence: Mia gave her friend some advice.
items.answer: Mia gave some advice to her friend.

items.sentence: Dad bought me a new backpack.
items.answer: Dad bought a new backpack for me.

items.sentence: The teacher showed us the answers.
items.answer: The teacher showed the answers to us.

items.sentence: Leo made his sister a sandwich.
items.answer: Leo made a sandwich for his sister.

items.sentence: I sent my aunt a photo.
items.answer: I sent a photo to my aunt.

items.sentence: They offered me a job.
items.answer: They offered a job to me.

sections.title: D: Gap fill (direct and indirect objects)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Please pass ...... the salt.
Could you read this email ...... me?
My uncle taught ...... how to drive.
We paid ...... waiter before leaving.
She wrote a long letter ...... her cousin.
Can you get a glass of water ...... me, please?

sections.title: E: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct sentence.

items.sentence: Tom gave to me his old bike.
items.answer: Tom gave me his old bike.

items.sentence: She bought to her son a new coat.
items.answer: She bought her son a new coat.

items.sentence: Please explain me the rules.
items.answer: Please explain the rules to me.

items.sentence: They offered to us some coffee.
I sent to my friend an invitation.
items.answer: I sent my friend an invitation.

items.sentence: The teacher read to us the story.
items.answer: The teacher read us the story.

sections.title: F: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning.

items.sentence:
I bought my sister a notebook. **FOR**
I bought a notebook ...... sister.

She gave a present to Tom. **GAVE**
She ...... present.

Could you send this email to me? **ME**
Could you ...... email?

He made lunch for us. **MADE**
He ...... lunch.

The guide showed the museum to the tourists. **SHOWED**
The guide ...... museum.

Don't give your password to anyone. **GIVE**
Don't ...... password.
